title: Paramount Wins Warner Bros. Now What? Plus: ‘Scream 7,’ ‘Heat 2,’ and ‘Nirvanna the Band the Show the Movie’ With Matt Johnson!
format: Sean Fennessey on what Paramount's win over Netflix for Warner Bros. means for movies. Chris Ryan on the Heat 2 cast and a Scream 7 he calls the worst of the franchise. Then Nirvanna the Band the Show the Movie with Amanda Dobbins and Adam Naiman, plus an interview with its director, Matt Johnson.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: A Skydance high point Sean cites for David Ellison's studio.

films.title: True Grit
films.note: The other Skydance credit in the studio's favor.

films.title: Heart of Stone
films.note: Filed under Skydance's forgettable big-budget streaming action.

films.title: The Adam Project
films.note: Same bucket, the low end of Skydance's output.

films.title: Heat 2
films.note: Christian Bale joins Leonardo DiCaprio; the guess is Bale as Vincent Hanna, Leo as young Neil McCauley. Chris, the book's real lead, remains uncast.

films.title: Heat
films.note: The Mann original the sequel-prequel is chasing, and, they worry, partly retreading.

films.title: The Godfather Part II
films.note: The De Niro/Pacino precedent for finally pairing Bale and DiCaprio.

films.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
films.note: Alex Ross Perry's comp: a long-running property recast decades on, and pulled off.

films.title: Star Wars: Episode I - The Phantom Menace
films.note: The other comp: same world, new actors, but a more complicated legacy.

films.title: Miami Vice
films.note: Where the Chris strand of Heat 2 overlaps Mann's own back catalog.

films.title: Blackhat
films.note: The other Mann film the Chris material effectively retreads.

films.title: The Bride!
films.note: Bale's imminent turn as Frankenstein's monster for Maggie Gyllenhaal; Sean's seeing it Monday, hears it's not good.

films.title: Scream 7
films.note: Chris's least favorite Scream: flat and self-serious, plays like a vigilante-justice movie on Paramount+. Neve Campbell back center-frame; Kevin Williamson returns to write. Killers: Ethan Embry and Anna Camp.

films.title: Scream
films.note: Still the best: Craven's control of space, the mystery, the Nick Cave 'Red Right Hand' town-shutdown Sean got chills at as a 13-year-old.

films.title: Scream 2
films.note: The film-class sequel; the Randy surrogate explains the rules.

films.title: Scream 3
films.note: The Hollywood one, about the Stab movies and how you'd tell the story. Chris rates it above 2.

films.title: Scream 4
films.note: Sean's favorite cold open: the Russian-nesting-doll of girls watching Stab, plus Mikey Madison's unhinged energy.

films.title: Scream (2022)
films.note: The requel Sean rewatched. Jack Quaid's killer, the start of the new trilogy Barrera and Ortega led.

films.title: Scream VI
films.note: Chris warmed to on revisit: stabby, giallo, not the NYC movie he wanted. Scream 7 takes several pointed shots at it.

films.title: I Know What You Did Last Summer
films.note: Williamson's other reboot, straining just as hard to feel contemporary and coming up short.

segments.heading: Nirvanna the Band the Show the Movie
segments.note: with Amanda Dobbins and Adam Naiman

films.title: Nirvanna the Band the Show the Movie
films.note: Sean's favorite movie of 2026 so far: a chaotic Toronto buddy comedy from Matt Johnson and Jay McCarroll that turns the CN Tower into a Mission: Impossible-meets-jackass prop. A time-travel movie about not making progress.

films.title: BlackBerry
films.note: Johnson's breakout; the Matt-ness compartmentalized into his own performance. Easily one of Sean's five favorite conversations on the show.

films.title: The Dirties
films.note: Johnson's first feature: an edgelord school-shooter movie Adam calls frightening, and the mean-spirited root of the Nirvana project.

films.title: Operation Avalanche
films.note: The one where they broke into NASA; Johnson filmed himself shaking hands with Kubrick.

films.title: Nirvanna the Band the Show the Movie
films.note: Built backwards out of one old DV clip; twice as effective, he argues, on people who know nothing. A last-chance movie made only with friends in Toronto.

films.title: Back to the Future
films.note: The film the characters treat as scripture; movies as the new religious language.

films.title: Streetwise
films.note: His last-great-thing: the doc he watched over and over to steal its far-away, wide-lens shooting style for public scenes.

films.title: F for Fake
films.note: Watched on a tiny computer and felt was made just for him: 'literally instructions for me.'

films.title: Whiplash
films.note: His example of the light going on: 'that's true,' real even though it didn't happen.

films.title: The Talented Mr. Ripley
films.note: The homage behind the first, abandoned version of the movie, shot for two months in New Orleans.

films.title: Timecode
films.note: The real-time, split-screen model for a possible next Nirvana movie.

films.title: Tony
films.note: His next film, for A24: a 19-year-old Anthony Bourdain, with Dominic Sessa. A deeply personal movie, and a hard shoot away from his Toronto crew.
